title: Hexagonal Service Pattern
description: Enforces hexagonal (ports-and-adapters) architecture where dependencies flow inward. Domain core has zero infrastructure imports. Infrastructure adapters implement domain-defined ports.

book-references: Cloud Native Patterns Ch. 6 — Stateless Processes
Head First Design Patterns Ch. 7 — Adapter Pattern
Head First Design Patterns Ch. 1 — Strategy Pattern

nodes.name: Web Client
nodes.description: Frontend application (SPA, dashboard) that consumes the API layer

nodes.name: API Layer
nodes.description: REST controllers and DTOs. Thin translation layer — no business logic. Validates input, delegates to handlers, maps responses.

nodes.name: Application Layer
nodes.description: CQRS handlers, schedulers, application services. Stateless, @Transactional. Orchestrates domain operations and publishes events.

nodes.name: Domain Core
nodes.description: Models, ports (interfaces), domain services, events. ZERO infrastructure imports. Defines the ubiquitous language and business rules.

nodes.name: Infrastructure Layer
nodes.description: JPA adapters, Kafka publisher, insurer adapters, config beans. Implements domain ports using specific technologies.

nodes.name: Primary Database
nodes.description: Persistent storage for domain state. Accessed only through repository adapters.

nodes.name: Cache
nodes.description: Distributed cache for read-heavy configuration data. @Cacheable with TTL.

relationships.description: Web client calls REST API endpoints
Handlers use domain models and call domain ports (interfaces)
Infrastructure adapters implement domain-defined port interfaces. Dependency inversion — infrastructure depends on domain, never the reverse.
JPA repository adapters persist and query domain state
Cache adapter stores frequently-read configuration data

constraints.description: Domain core must have ZERO imports from infrastructure packages (no jakarta.persistence, no org.springframework.kafka, no org.springframework.data)
API depends on Application. Application depends on Domain. Infrastructure depends on Domain. Domain depends on nothing external.
All @Service handlers must have only private final fields (no mutable instance state)
Database is accessed only through JPA repository adapters implementing domain ports. No direct JDBC in handlers.
